title: I benchmarked every PDF-to-Markdown tool. Then I built a router.
description: No single PDF extraction tool wins at everything. I tested PyMuPDF, Docling, Marker, Surya, and Gemini Flash across 4 categories — then built pdfmux.

title: How to give your AI agent the ability to read any PDF
description: Step-by-step tutorial for adding PDF processing to Claude, Cursor, or any AI agent using pdfmux's built-in MCP server.
notes: MCP ecosystem growing fast, thin content exists. Definitive tutorial opportunity.

title: What 'self-healing' PDF extraction actually looks like
description: How pdfmux audits every page, detects broken extraction, and re-extracts automatically — with real before/after examples.
notes: Core differentiator. Explains the audit pipeline with real data. Also serves as contributor on-ramp.

title: Which PDF extractor should you use? An honest guide
description: An honest comparison of PyMuPDF, Docling, Marker, Surya, Unstructured, and LlamaParse — when to use each, with real benchmarks.
target_keywords: which pdf extractor to use python
notes: Decision guide. Captures comparison keywords. Honest about when NOT to use pdfmux.

title: 90% of your PDFs convert in 10ms. Here's what to do about the other 10%.
description: Most PDFs are digital and extract instantly. The hard part is the 10% that need OCR. Here's how pdfmux handles both automatically.
notes: The 90/10 insight. Viral potential — surprising stat that reframes the problem.

title: pdfmux + LangChain: RAG pipeline with per-page confidence scores
description: Build a RAG pipeline that knows which pages to trust. LangChain integration with pdfmux's confidence scoring for reliable retrieval.
notes: Integration tutorial. Drives LangChain ecosystem traffic. Companion to integration PR.

title: I processed 1,000 PDFs with pdfmux. Here's what the confidence data reveals.
description: Real data from processing 1,000 PDFs — confidence score distributions, extractor routing patterns, and what breaks most often.
notes: Competitive moat content. Only pdfmux can publish routing decision data. HN potential.

title: The hidden cost of using one PDF tool for everything
description: Using a single PDF extractor means silent failures on 10-15% of documents. Here's the data on what you're missing.
notes: Pain-point content. Targets developers whose pipelines silently fail.

title: Extract tables from PDF in Python — zero config
description: How to extract tables from PDFs in Python with 97.9% accuracy using pdfmux. Auto-routes to Docling for tables, PyMuPDF for everything else.
notes: High-volume keyword. Practical tutorial that shows the routing advantage.

title: Processing scanned PDFs in Python: the complete guide
description: The complete guide to extracting text from scanned PDFs in Python — OCR setup, accuracy optimization, and handling mixed digital/scanned documents.
notes: Long-tail capture. Targets developers with scanned document problems.

title: LlamaIndex + pdfmux: document processing for RAG
description: Build a LlamaIndex RAG pipeline with pdfmux as the document reader. Per-page confidence scores flow through to your vector store.
notes: LlamaIndex ecosystem traffic. Companion to integration PR.

title: Chunking PDFs for RAG: getting the boundaries right
description: Why naive text splitting destroys RAG accuracy. How pdfmux's section-aware chunking preserves document structure with token estimates.
notes: RAG-specific content. Targets the chunking pain point.

title: pdfmux vs Unstructured vs LlamaParse vs Docling: a real comparison
description: Honest feature and performance comparison of pdfmux, Unstructured, LlamaParse, and Docling for PDF extraction in production pipelines.
notes: Multi-competitor comparison. Captures cross-competitor search traffic.

title: Which Python PDF library should you use in 2026?
description: A developer's guide to choosing the right Python PDF library in 2026 — from simple text extraction to full LLM pipeline integration.
target_keywords: best python library for pdf extraction 2026
notes: Annual roundup. Time to PyCon content sprint.

title: Why your PDF extraction is broken (and how to fix it)
description: The 5 most common reasons PDF extraction fails silently — and how to detect and fix each one with confidence scoring.
notes: Pain-point content. Targets frustrated developers searching for solutions.

title: We don't compete with Docling, Marker, or PyMuPDF. We use them.
description: Why pdfmux routes to the best extractor per page instead of trying to be one. The meta-tool approach to PDF extraction.
notes: Brand narrative. The meta-tool argument. Disarms competitor comparisons.
